examples.title: On-Demand Resource Provisioning
examples.description: Demonstrates how cloud resources can be provisioned on-demand based on user request
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ProvisionResource(resourceType, size)
BEGIN
    IF ValidateUserRequest(userID, resourceType) THEN
        BEGIN
            availableResources ← CheckResourcePool(resourceType)
            IF availableResources >= size THEN
                BEGIN
                    AllocateResources(userID, resourceType, size)
                    GenerateBillingMeter(userID, resourceType)
                    RETURN "Resource provisioned successfully"
                END
            ELSE
                BEGIN
                    ScaleUpResources(resourceType, size)
                    AllocateResources(userID, resourceType, size)
                    RETURN "Resources scaled and provisioned"
                END
        END
    ELSE
        RETURN "Request validation failed"
    END
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the on-demand self-service characteristic where users can automatically provision resources. The system validates the request, checks resource availability in the pool, and either allocates existing resources or scales up to meet demand.

examples.title: Elastic Resource Scaling Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates automatic scaling based on demand metrics
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates rapid elasticity - the ability to automatically scale resources based on demand. It scales up when demand increases and scales down when demand decreases, optimizing costs while maintaining performance.

examples.title: Multi-Tenant Resource Allocation
examples.description: Demonstrates how cloud providers allocate shared resources to multiple tenants
examples.code:
PROCEDURE AllocateToTenant(tenantID, resourceRequest)
BEGIN
    tenantConfig ← GetTenantConfiguration(tenantID)
    
    // Check resource quota for this tenant
    IF tenantConfig.usedResources + resourceRequest <= tenantConfig.quota THEN
        BEGIN
            // Create virtual resource partition
            virtualPartition ← CreateVirtualPartition()
            
            // Apply tenant-specific isolation policies
            ApplyNetworkIsolation(tenantID)
            ApplyStorageEncryption(tenantID)
            
            // Allocate resources from the pool
            physicalResources ← SelectFromResourcePool(resourceRequest)
            MapVirtualToPhysical(virtualPartition, physicalResources)
            
            UpdateTenantUsage(tenantID, resourceRequest)
            RETURN virtualPartition
        END
    ELSE
        RETURN "Quota exceeded - upgrade required"
    END
END

PROCEDURE GetTenantConfiguration(tenantID)
BEGIN
    RETURN SELECT * FROM Tenants WHERE tenantID = tenantID
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates multi-tenancy where the cloud provider allocates shared physical resources to multiple tenants while maintaining isolation. Each tenant gets virtual partitions with network isolation and storage encryption to ensure security and privacy.

examples.title: Pay-Per-Use Billing System
examples.description: Demonstrates measured service characteristic with usage tracking and billing
examples.explanation: This Java code demonstrates the measured service characteristic of cloud computing. It tracks resource usage per tenant and calculates bills based on the rate per unit for different services (compute, storage, bandwidth), enabling transparent pay-per-use pricing.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of service types
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of tenant-service combinations
